decision.reason: The Snowflake stable surface certifies SQL warehouse, staged-file batch ingestion, hosted procedure execution, task graph execution and control-table evidence. Snowpipe, Streams, Snowpipe Streaming and Kafka connector ingestion require a separate connector/runtime design with different recovery, offset and evidence semantics.
decision.planner_behavior: Keep these sources REVIEW_REQUIRED or UNSUPPORTED for the Snowflake SQL warehouse runtime. Do not silently fall back to staged-file batch ingestion.
decision.certified_alternative: Use staged_files with named CSV, JSON or Parquet file formats for the current Snowflake stable surface.

official_reference.name: Snowflake Connector for Kafka
official_reference.note: Snowflake documents Kafka ingestion as a connector that reads Kafka topics and loads Snowflake tables.

official_reference.name: Snowpipe Streaming
official_reference.note: Snowpipe Streaming is a separate ingestion API path from SQL warehouse batch contracts.

required_before_promotion: Define a Snowflake connector/runtime mapping for Kafka/Snowpipe/Streams that preserves ContractForge available-now or streaming intent.
Record offset/checkpoint or channel state semantics in ContractForge evidence tables.
Validate first-run, no-input rerun, failure/redaction and cost/query-history evidence on a live Snowflake account.
Document any semantic difference from Databricks and AWS available-now stream behavior.
